compiled_state.title: dso plugin-registered agents not available as Agent tool subagent_type — inline fallback used instead
compiled_state.author: Test

comments.body:
Observed during implementation-plan execution for ec03-5076: the dso:approach-decision-maker agent was dispatched using the inline fallback path — Agent tool with subagent_type='general-purpose' and the agent definition file read as the prompt. The Claude Code Agent tool does not accept 'dso:approach-decision-maker' as a valid subagent_type value.

The plugin registers 29 agents (confirmed by /reload-plugins output). However, these are not recognized as valid subagent_type values by the Claude Code Agent tool. CLAUDE.md documents this limitation with the workaround: 'Always dispatch DSO named agents by reading plugins/dso/agents/<agent-name>.md inline and passing the content as the task prompt with subagent_type: general-purpose and the agent specified model.'

This is a known architectural gap: the plugin agent registry does not integrate with the Claude Code Agent tool's subagent_type routing. The inline fallback works but loses: (1) any specialized routing/configuration the plugin system provides for named agents, (2) model-level enforcement (agents specifying model: opus can be bypassed by choosing a different subagent_type), (3) visibility into which named agent was actually dispatched.

The /reload-plugins command shows '29 agents' are registered — clarify whether these are expected to be dispatchable via subagent_type or if the inline dispatch is intended to be permanent.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
Investigation findings (fix-bug workflow):

CLASSIFICATION: working-as-designed — no code fix possible

Root cause: The Claude Code Agent tool's  parameter only accepts built-in types (general-purpose, Explore, Plan, etc.). This is an API-level constraint of Claude Code, not a plugin bug.

CLAUDE.md line 77 explicitly documents: 'Agent dispatch — inline is the primary path: The Agent tool's subagent_type parameter only accepts built-in types (general-purpose, Explore, Plan, etc.) — dso:* values are not valid. Always dispatch DSO named agents by reading plugins/dso/agents/<agent-name>.md inline.'

Clarification: Inline dispatch IS the intended permanent approach. dso:* values as subagent_type are NOT expected to work — this is by design. The plugin's 'registered agents' in the plugin system are discoverable for orchestrators to READ inline (as .md files), not for routing via the subagent_type parameter.

No code change is possible to make dso:* work as subagent_type values without changes to the Claude Code Agent tool itself. Leaving open per protocol (cannot close without code change or explicit user request).
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation: working-as-designed, no code fix possible. Claude Code Agent tool subagent_type only accepts built-in types. CLAUDE.md line 77 explicitly states inline dispatch is the primary path and dso:* values are not valid. The plugin registered agents are read inline as .md prompts, not routed via subagent_type. No code change possible without Claude Code API changes. Leaving open per protocol.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigated (debug-everything session): working-as-designed — no code fix possible. Evidence: CLAUDE.md explicitly documents inline dispatch as the intended permanent approach. The Claude Code Agent tool's subagent_type parameter only accepts built-in types (general-purpose, Explore, Plan, etc.) by design. dso:* agent types are intended to be dispatched by reading the agent .md files inline and using subagent_type='general-purpose'. No architectural change is possible without changes to the Claude Code Agent tool itself. Leaving OPEN per debug-everything protocol (no code change = no close).
comments.author: Test
